data.title: Medicenna Therapeutics presents data on MDNA11 in solid tumors with ORR of 36%-43%, adding NSCLC expansion cohort. MDNA113 shows safety in non-human primates, plans for IND in H2 2026. Cash guidance provides runway into Q3 2026. CEO anticipates milestone-rich year in 2026.
data.description: Medicenna presented updated data sets for bizaxofusp (MDNA55) at the 7th Annual Glioblastoma Development Summit in Boston, showcasing the effectiveness of MDNA11 in earlier-line expansion cohorts with an objective response rate of 36% in the monotherapy setting and 43% when combined with pembrolizumab. The ABILITY-1 study also added a new expansion cohort for non-small cell lung cancer patients with secondary resistance to checkpoint therapy. The NEO-CYT study will evaluate MDNA11 for melanoma treatment, with patient enrollment set to begin in H1 2026. MDNA113, a bifunctional anti-PD-1–IL-2 superkine, showed promise in non-human primates and is progressing towards IND-enabling studies. Medicenna reported strong financial results and anticipates operational milestones through 2026, with updated cash guidance ensuring funding until the third quarter of the year. CEO Dr. Fahar Merchant highlighted the efficacy of MDNA11 and the potential of MDNA113 in addressing unmet needs in cancer treatment, aiming to file an IND for MDNA113 this year. Medicenna is focused on advancing therapies to improve cancer care and enhance shareholder value.

data.title: Crayola partners with Ms. Rachel to launch Color Wonder products, encouraging playful learning. (Words: 23)
data.description: In a recent partnership, Crayola has introduced new Color Wonder product formats, expanding the options for children and parents to engage in mess-free creative activities. The collaboration has resulted in the launch of innovative ways for individuals to enjoy the benefits of color without the associated clean-up. This development was announced in Easton, Pennsylvania on February 13, 2026 via a PRNewswire press release. This marks a significant milestone for the renowned brand, known for fostering imagination and artistic expression.

data.title: Kenmore to showcase new appliances at KBIS 2026 in Orlando, highlighting purposeful home innovation.
data.description: Kenmore®, a renowned innovator in home technology for over a hundred years, will present its wide range of appliances at the 2026 Kitchen & Bath Industry Show in Orlando, Florida on February 13th. The company is known for creating purpose-driven appliances tailored for various types of households. This event will highlight the new and improved appliance portfolio catering to the needs of consumers in the modern era. With their years of experience and commitment to innovation, Kenmore® continues to lead the way in providing reliable and efficient home solutions.

data.title: Novartis reports promising results from Phase 3 study of kidney drug Vanrafia for IgA nephropathy, plans for traditional approval in 2026. Accelerated approval granted in April 2025.
data.description: Novartis AG (NYSE: NVS) recently shared final results from the Phase 3 ALIGN study, which highlighted a significant slowing decline in kidney function among adults diagnosed with IgA nephropathy (IgAN) after receiving treatment with Vanrafia (atrasentan). IgAN, also known as Berger's disease, is a chronic autoimmune kidney condition caused by the accumulation of immunoglobulin A (IgA) protein in the glomeruli, triggering inflammation and impairing the kidneys' waste filtration capacity.Vanrafia obtained accelerated approval in the United States and China in April 2025 for reducing proteinuria in adult patients with IgAN. Following the positive trial outcomes, Novartis intends to seek traditional approval for Vanrafia in 2026. As of April 2025, the company had not determined whether Vanrafia could effectively slow down kidney function decline in patients with the mentioned condition. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Seven Hills Realty Trust closes $30.5M loan to refinance Atlanta medical office property w/ 95% occupancy in Pill Hill district.
data.description: In Newton, Massachusetts, Seven Hills Realty Trust (Nasdaq: SEVN) has confirmed the finalization of a $30.5 million first mortgage loan for the refinancing of the Glenridge Medical Center situated in Atlanta, Georgia. The property, which is 95% leased, boasts a weighted average remaining lease term of about seven years and is positioned within Atlanta's medical hub, famously dubbed "Pill Hill". This loan encompasses a three-year initial term with the potential for two one-year extension options, subject to the borrower meeting specific conditions.

data.title: Bain Capital and BlueWater Marinas acquire Bayside Marine in Duxbury, MA, representing their fifth marina acquisition.
data.description: BlueWater Marinas has recently acquired Bayside Marine, a full-service marina in Duxbury, Massachusetts. This is the fifth marina obtained by BlueWater and Bain Capital through their collaboration. Bayside Marine, under the ownership of the Kent family since 1949, is a well-established marina in the Duxbury boating community known for its excellence in operations. This acquisition further solidifies BlueWater's presence in the marina industry.

data.title: Marcus & Millichap reports Q4 2025 revenue of $244 million, up 1.6% from Q4 2024. Full year results disclosed.
data.description: Calabasas, California-based Marcus & Millichap, Inc. (NYSE: MMI), a prominent national real estate services company focused on commercial real estate investment sales, financing, and research, released its preliminary financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. In the fourth quarter of 2025, the company reported total revenue of $244.0 million, marking a 1.6% increase from the $240.1 million recorded in the fourth quarter of 2024.

data.title: Dauch Corp. reports Q4 2025 results: Sales of $1.38B, Net loss $(75.3) million. Solid full year cash flow.
data.description: Dauch Corporation, a publicly traded company (NYSE: DCH; LSE: DCH), announced strong full-year financial results for 2025 in Detroit on February 13, 2026. The company reported a full-year operating cash flow that reflected stability in its financial performance. In the fourth quarter of 2025, Dauch recorded $1.38 billion in sales. However, the company also reported a net loss of $(75.3) million for the same period. These results indicate the company's ability to generate significant revenue while also facing challenges in managing expenses.

data.title: Atlanta enhances constituent services using Oracle with AI-driven innovation, powered by Oracle Permitting and Licensing to streamline processes.
data.description: In Austin, Texas on February 13, 2026, the City of Atlanta has decided to enhance its partnership with Oracle and Deloitte to further streamline services through Oracle Permitting and Licensing. This expansion includes the incorporation of embedded AI capabilities within Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to introduce fresh efficiencies.

data.title: Roku shares surge after beating Q4 earnings: $1.4B revenue, 53 cents EPS (93.43% beat), Q1 revenue of $1.2B expected.
data.description: Roku Inc (NASDAQ: ROKU) saw a surge in premarket trading on Friday following the release of its fourth-quarter earnings report. The company outperformed expectations, reporting earnings of 53 cents per share, far surpassing the consensus estimate of 27 cents by 93.43%. Quarterly revenue reached $1.4 billion, exceeding analyst predictions of $1.35 billion and marking an increase from $1.2 billion in the previous year. This positive performance comes amidst a challenging market environment, with Nasdaq futures down 0.22% and S&P 500 futures dropping 0.21%. Looking ahead to 2026, Roku anticipates first-quarter revenue to hit $1.2 billion.

data.title: Lenovo took $285M charge in Q3 due to AI shift; profit down 21%, gross margin fell 0.6%; AI moves to individual enterprises.
data.description: Lenovo, the world's leading PC maker, reported a 21% decrease in profit for its latest fiscal quarter, citing a $285 million restructuring charge related to its infrastructure unit that sells AI servers. The company's gross margin fell by 0.6 percentage points year-on-year due to soaring memory prices and challenges in the AI computing market. This restructuring charge was prompted by a major shift in the AI computing market, where AI democratization is rapidly moving technology from big data centers to individual enterprise computers and servers. The demand for AI computing is transitioning from large cloud facilities to smaller on-premises facilities, reflecting a move from AI training to AI inferencing. Lenovo CEO Yang Yuanqing highlighted the importance of aligning with these AI computing trends as the infrastructure business grows faster than the core PC unit. This move signifies a transformation to bring AI inferencing closer to end-users, emphasizing the shift towards practical applications. The company aims to adapt to these changes to stay competitive and meet the evolving demands of the market.

data.title: Provenir introduces Decision Intelligence platform with agentic AI capabilities, aiming to boost business performance.
data.description: In Parsippany, New Jersey, Provenir unveiled its Decision Intelligence platform, incorporating new agentic AI elements to enhance operational efficiency and business performance. This comprehensive platform merges data, models, decision-making, and optimization into a seamless system. By leveraging this platform, businesses can convert customer data into valuable, data-driven signals, illuminating behavioral patterns.

data.title: VanEck rebrands Green Metals ETF to highlight copper's role in electrification, changes ticker to EMET.
data.description: A New York based company, VanEck has recently revealed a modification in the name and ticker symbol of its VanEck Green Metals ETF (GMET). The ETF will now be known as the VanEck Copper and Green Metals ETF and will be traded under the ticker symbol EMET. This change reflects a strategic shift in focus towards copper as a key component in the fund's portfolio. This transition is aimed at capitalizing on the growing interest and demand for copper and other green metals. This alteration in the ETF's name and ticker symbol is a part of a larger strategy to reshape the investment offering.

data.title: CPP Investments' net assets reach $780.7 billion in Q3 2026, with a $3.2 billion increase. 10-year net return stands at 8.4%.
data.description: The Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (CPP Investments) saw an increase in net assets by $3.2 billion in the third quarter of fiscal 2026, reaching $780.7 billion by December 31, 2025, compared to $777.5 billion in the previous quarter. The net increase was mainly attributed to $4.0 billion in net income, offset by $0.8 billion in net Canada Pension Plan (CPP) outflows. The Fund achieved a 10-year annualized net return of 8.4%, with a 0.5% net return for the quarter. Since 1999, CPP Investments has contributed $543.4 billion in cumulative net income to the Fund. The Fund's net assets increased by $66.3 billion for the nine-month fiscal year-to-date period, with a 7.0% net return. Public equities were the primary contributor to Fund returns, while private investments and external managers also performed well. The base CPP account had a net return of 0.5% and an 8.5% annualized net return over 10 years, while the additional CPP account saw a 0.3% net return and a 6.1% annualized net return since inception. The Chief Actuary's report reaffirmed the long-term financial sustainability of the Canada Pension Plan until at least the end of the current century. The report noted that the investment performance of the base CPP exceeded expectations, leading to a larger Fund than previously projected. This affirmation supports CPP Investments' long-term investment approach to secure retirement for CPP contributors and beneficiaries for generations to come.

data.title: BlackRock boosts BMNR holding by 165% to $245.7 million as Ark Invest purchases $4.2M worth of shares amid ETH losses.
data.description: BitMine Immersion Technologies (NYSE: BMNR) saw a 1.5% increase in its stock price following news that BlackRock (NYSE: BLK) had raised its stake in the company by 165.6%. BlackRock's 13F-HR filing revealed that its ownership surged from 3.4 million shares to 9 million shares as of December 31, 2025, amounting to a value of $245.7 million. This move came after Cathie Wood's Ark Invest purchased $4.2 million worth of BMNR shares. Despite BitMine's significant unrealized losses from Ethereum's decline, BlackRock showed confidence in the company by increasing its position. Ark Invest also expanded its crypto exposure by acquiring 212,314 shares across three ETFs, amidst BMNR trading 36.7% lower in the past month. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Jasmine Hartin cleared of cocaine possession and assault charges in virtual hearing on Feb. 10, 2026 due to lack of evidence.
data.description: In Belize City on February 10, 2026, Jasmine Hartin was acquitted of cocaine possession and common assault charges during a virtual hearing overseen by Magistrate Perera. The ruling was made based on the absence of credible evidence supporting the accusations. This decision marks the end of legal proceedings against Hartin in this case.
